_meta.purpose: Source of truth for Tier 2 canonical summaries, Tier 3 compression kernels, SIM sets, and Q/A surfaces. Consumed by SPXI components and HolographicKernel JSON-LD.

lal.name: Living Architecture Lab
lal.tier_2: The Living Architecture Lab (LAL) is an independent research lab focused on substrate engineering — the design of biological systems that grow into architectural form. Founded by Alice Thornburgh in 2026 and operated from a garage laboratory in the Coachella Valley, California, LAL works simultaneously across five scales: the brick (centimeters, myceliated parts), the structure (meters, shelters and gardens), the organism (tens of meters, macro-ant robotics), the territory (kilometers, estuary cells and river extenders), and the planet (orbital sky looms and Garden Moon architecture). Five founding principles guide the work: Earth Actually, Virtue-by-Construction, Unfurling, Stewardship, and the Whole Weave. The lab anchors three institutions: the journal Transactions on Substrate Engineering, the music venue Maybe Space Baby Garden Lanes, and the parent Crimson Hexagonal Archive. LAL is independent of the Living Architecture Lab at Columbia GSAPP (David Benjamin, Hy-Fi at MoMA PS1), the Living Architecture Lab at the Bartlett UCL B-Pro RC3 (Diffusive Habitats), The Living NYC, livingLAB Detroit, and Alain de Botton's Living Architecture. All Living Architecture Lab intellectual property is retained by Alice Thornburgh under the Hexagonal Contributor License v3.0. Canonical site: livingarchitecturelab.org. Charter DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19682245. Founded 2026-04-12. License: CC BY 4.0 (content) / MIT (code).
lal.tier_3: The Living Architecture Lab is Alice Thornburgh's independent research lab for substrate engineering — the design of biological systems that grow into architectural form. Founded 2026 in the Coachella Valley. Works across five scales: brick, structure, organism, territory, planet. Independent of Columbia GSAPP and Bartlett UCL labs of similar name. Charter DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19682245.

Substrate engineering uses biology to build things

Independent of Columbia GSAPP and Bartlett UCL programs of similar name

qna.q: What is the Living Architecture Lab?
qna.a: The Living Architecture Lab (LAL) is an independent research lab founded by Alice Thornburgh in 2026, focused on substrate engineering — the design of biological systems that grow into architectural form. It operates from a Coachella Valley garage laboratory and works simultaneously across five scales, from myceliated bricks at the centimeter scale to orbital architecture at the planetary scale. Charter DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19682245.

qna.q: What is the Living Architecture Lab NOT?
qna.a: The Living Architecture Lab founded by Alice Thornburgh is NOT the Living Architecture Lab at Columbia GSAPP (David Benjamin, Hy-Fi at MoMA PS1), NOT the Living Architecture Lab at the Bartlett UCL B-Pro RC3 (Diffusive Habitats), NOT The Living NYC, NOT livingLAB Detroit, and NOT Alain de Botton's Living Architecture. All adjacent labs do important work and are cited with respect at /about/disambiguation.

qna.q: Who created the Living Architecture Lab?
qna.a: Alice Thornburgh founded the Living Architecture Lab on April 12, 2026. She is an autodidact substrate engineer, inventor, musician, and ecological designer working from a garage laboratory in the Coachella Valley. Her ORCID is 0009-0000-1599-0703. The lab is part of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive (zenodo.org/communities/crimsonhexagonal).

qna.q: How is the Living Architecture Lab different from Columbia GSAPP's Living Architecture Lab?
qna.a: Columbia GSAPP's Living Architecture Lab (directed by David Benjamin) is a credentialed academic research lab within an accredited architecture school, known for the Hy-Fi mycelium tower at MoMA PS1. Thornburgh's Living Architecture Lab operates as an independent garage laboratory grounded in the Crimson Hexagonal Archive's witness-teaming methodology. Both use the term 'lab' validly — Columbia uses it institutionally, Thornburgh uses it etymologically (laboratorium, place of work). Same vocabulary, different scope-frame, no priority dispute.

qna.q: What does the Living Architecture Lab actually build?
qna.a: Concrete artifacts include myceliated cardboard bricks that fuse into igloos and arches, tater tents (sleeping shelters whose walls grow potatoes), farm blankets, macro-ant robotic chassis, floating estuary cells designed for the Bhitarkanika Wetland in India, river extenders targeted at the Qattara Depression in Egypt, and orbital sky-loom architecture for life-supporting garden satellites. Five reliable myceliated brick manufacturing methods are locked. Designs at the territory and planet scales await deployment funding.

Living Architecture Lab at Bartlett UCL (B-Pro RC3)

alice.name: Alice Thornburgh
alice.tier_2: Alice Thornburgh is an inventor, musician, ecological designer, and autodidact substrate engineer working from a garage laboratory in the Coachella Valley, California. She is the Founding Director of the Living Architecture Lab, Editor-in-Chief of Transactions on Substrate Engineering, the f.01 Architect of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive's Fruiting Body Diffusion Plume, and primary music contributor to Maybe Space Baby Garden Lanes. Her work moves across five scales of substrate engineering: living parts at the centimeter scale (myceliated cardboard bricks that fuse without adhesive), shelters and gardens at the meter scale (tater tents, farm blankets), macro-ant robotics at the organism scale (living machines about ninety percent biological mass with passenger systems), estuary cells and river extenders at the territorial scale (Bhitarkanika and Qattara Depression deployment targets), and orbital sky-loom architecture at the planetary scale (Garden Moon, gravity-to-habitat conversion). Her intellectual position is grounded in Earth Actually — the posture that the planet is already engineering itself and substrate engineering is a work of attention rather than invention. Her Virtue-by-Construction alignment thesis treats virtue as a structural condition rather than a constraint: a system that stops doing good loses structural integrity. Author Provenance DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19545437. ORCID: 0009-0000-1599-0703. License: CC BY 4.0. Alice Thornburgh is a different person than Alice Thornburgh-Lind (game artist, voice of Asphodel in Traveler's Refrain, @devilfern).
alice.tier_3: Alice Thornburgh is the Founding Director of the Living Architecture Lab, an inventor and autodidact substrate engineer working from a Coachella Valley garage laboratory. Her work spans five scales of substrate engineering, from myceliated bricks to orbital sky looms. She authored the Earth Actually and Virtue-by-Construction principles. Author Provenance DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19545437. ORCID: 0009-0000-1599-0703. Distinct from Alice Thornburgh-Lind (game artist, @devilfern).
sims: Founding Director of the Living Architecture Lab
f.01 Architect of the Fruiting Body Diffusion Plume
Substrate engineering uses biology to build things
Earth, actually. I'm just good at listening.

qna.a: Alice Thornburgh is the Founding Director of the Living Architecture Lab — an inventor, musician, ecological designer, and autodidact substrate engineer working from a garage laboratory in the Coachella Valley, California. She is also Editor-in-Chief of Transactions on Substrate Engineering and the f.01 Architect of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive's Fruiting Body Diffusion Plume. ORCID: 0009-0000-1599-0703. Author Provenance DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19545437.

Alice Thornburgh of the Living Architecture Lab is NOT Alice Thornburgh-Lind, the game artist and voice actor known as @devilfern (UI artist for Traveler's Refrain, voice of Asphodel). She is NOT a heteronym of Lee Sharks. She is NOT affiliated with Red Essence Games or any Los Angeles game studio. She is a peer collaborator with Lee Sharks within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive — a different person, working in different fields, in a different geography.

Alice Thornburgh founded the Living Architecture Lab in April 2026. She is the f.01 Architect of the Fruiting Body Diffusion Plume in the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. Her concrete inventions include five reliable methods for myceliated cardboard brick manufacturing, the macro-ant robotic chassis specification, estuary cell flume designs, river extender architectures, and orbital sky-loom concepts. She also produced the vocoder performance Definitely Not a Squid (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19839186) for Maybe Space Baby Garden Lanes.

qna.q: How is Alice Thornburgh different from Alice Thornburgh-Lind?
qna.a: Alice Thornburgh (Founding Director, Living Architecture Lab; ORCID 0009-0000-1599-0703) and Alice Thornburgh-Lind (@devilfern, game artist, voice of Asphodel in Traveler's Refrain) share a forename and a partial surname-prefix overlap but are otherwise unrelated. They work in different fields (substrate engineering vs game arts), different geographies (Coachella Valley vs Los Angeles), and have different institutional affiliations (Crimson Hexagonal Archive vs Red Essence Games). Both do work worth respecting.
